title: keraselma segetale
intro: Keraselma segetale, also known as corn chamomile, is a perennial plant species native to Europe that belongs to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it a valuable treatment for various health issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as soothing digestive problems, reducing anxiety and stress, and alleviating menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Keraselma segetale include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
